quick_skim.title: Advancing fronts for the thin-film equation with null slip and repulsive potentials: the case of partial wetting
quick_skim.abstract: For negative values of the spreading coefficient (that is, in the so-called ``partial wetting'' regime), we prove that the thin-film equation with zero slip and repulsive potentials $P$ of the form $P(h)\approx h^{1-m}$ as $h\to 0$, $m>1$, admits for any positive speed a one-parameter family of travelling-wave solutions with a contact line and (as in standard slippage models) a logarithmically-corrected linear behaviour as $h\to +\infty$. These waves have locally finite rate of dissipation for any $m>1$ and locally finite energy for any $m\in (1,3)$. The result thus confirms that mildly repulsive potentials effectively resolve the no-slip paradox. The family is parametrized by a thermodynamically consistent contact-line condition which reduces to the classical fixed microscopic contact-angle one if $P\equiv 0$.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Mathematical analysis of thin-film equation for partial wetting, directly relevant to falling film dynamics.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 薄液膜方程在部分润湿条件下的数学分析，直接与降膜动力学相关。
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 薄液膜方程在部分润湿条件下的数学分析，直接与降膜动力学相关。
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 薄液膜方程在部分润湿条件下的数学分析，直接与降膜动力学相关。
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper proves existence of travelling-wave solutions for the thin-film equation with zero slip and repulsive potentials in partial wetting, resolving the no-slip paradox and providing a model for falling film contact line motion.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对部分润湿条件下无滑移薄液膜方程，证明了排斥势能可有效解决无滑移悖论，并存在一类具有接触线的行进波解。该解具有对数校正的线性行为且能量耗散有限，为建立热力学一致的接触线模型提供了数学基础。这一分析对于理解降膜流动中的动态湿润现象具有重要理论意义。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对部分润湿条件下无滑移薄液膜方程，证明了排斥势能可有效解决无滑移悖论，并存在一类具有接触线的行进波解。该解具有对数校正的线性行为且能量耗散有限，为建立热力学一致的接触线模型提供了数学基础。这一分析对于理解降膜流动中的动态湿润现象具有重要理论意义。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 请帮我查找与传热管垂直降膜换热、CFD模拟相关的论文, especially work that connects or combines: heat transfer in falling film; falling film on vertical tube; two-phase flow simulation; liquid film thickness measurement; multiphase flow CFD; heat and mass transfer in falling film; CFD modeling of falling film heat transfer in vertical tubes; Numerical simulation of heat transfer in vertical falling film heat exchangers; Heat transfer characteristics of falling film over vertical tubes using computational fluid dynamics; Falling film evaporation and heat transfer in vertical tubes CFD.
